pi_items.source: Web search confirmed these are real cities in France. Paris is the capital in north-central France. Dijon is ~310 km southeast of Paris. Lyon is ~391 km southeast of Paris. These form a logical north-to-south route corridor. References: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lyon, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dijon

Verified via official Sygic documentation: 'Route' appears in Route overview/info screens (https://www.manula.com/manuals/sygic/sygic-gps-navigation/1/en/topic/starting-the-route). 'Stop' is used in 'Add stop' button per official manual (https://www.manula.com/manuals/sygic/sygic-gps-navigation-for-android-2-0/1/en/topic/adding-waypoint-in-route-overview). 'Waypoint' appears in menu options. 'km' used for distances per View & Units settings (https://www.manula.com/manuals/sygic/sygic-gps-navigation-for-android-2-0/1/en/topic/view-amp-units). Time displays in h (hours) and min (minutes) format.
Metadata claim is reasonable - at least 2 UI text matches from the expected_ui_text list would be typical for a completed route planning task in Sygic GPS.
Verified via Google Play Store (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sygic.aura). Sygic GPS Navigation & Maps is the official app with 95 million downloads and 4.17/5 rating.

pi_items.metadata_value: Search Lyon as destination, add Dijon as stop/waypoint, create Paris->Dijon->Lyon route
pi_items.verified_value: Sygic GPS supports this workflow: destination search, multi-waypoint routing, and route overview
pi_items.source: Official Sygic documentation shows route selection screen with destination and 'Add stop' options (https://www.manula.com/manuals/sygic/sygic-gps-navigation/1/en/topic/route-amp-navigation). Multi-waypoint itineraries are supported per developer docs (https://www.sygic.com/developers/professional-navigation-sdk/android/faq/how-to-use-itineraries).

privileged_info_summary: The task uses Sygic GPS Navigation (com.sygic.aura), a real navigation application with 95 million downloads available on Google Play. It operates on three real French cities: Paris (capital, north-central France), Dijon (~310 km southeast of Paris), and Lyon (~391 km southeast of Paris), which form a logical travel corridor. The expected UI text strings (Route, Stop, Waypoint, km, h, min) are all verified from official Sygic documentation and appear in the actual application interface. The task workflow—searching for destination, using 'Add stop' to create waypoints, and displaying route overview—is standard functionality confirmed in official manuals.
